Title: Targeted engineering of camelina and pennycress seeds for ultrahigh accumulation of acetyl-TAG
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing seeds at 20 DAF; expression not significantly affected by EfDAcT expression or fae1 mutation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seeds under standard growth conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: no detectable contribution established in this study; expression remained unchanged across genotypes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDAT1 transcript levels were measured to assess possible compensation in TAG synthesis. 2. PDAT1 expression was not affected by EfDAcT expression or fae1 mutation in pennycress. 3. The study therefore does not support PDAT1 as a main determinant of the acetyl-TAG phenotype.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: currently not prioritized based on this study alone
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: included because it was experimentally examined in Results, but it was not shown to causally drive the target metabolite phenotype

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: reduced-viscosity, lower-freezing-point designer oil for industrial and biofuel applications
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil bodies in cotyledons and embryonic axis of seeds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sn-3 acetylation branch of TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transgenically expressed in developing seeds; assayed in pennycress developing seeds at 20 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: seed-specific promoter(s), exact promoter not stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing and mature seeds under standard growth chamber conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers an acetate group from acetyl-CoA to the sn-3 position of diacylglycerol to form acetyl-TAG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key terminal determinant for acetyl-TAG synthesis; high-activity EfDAcT outperforms previous DAcT enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable transgenic seed expression in camelina and pennycress; metabolite profiling by TLC-GC/FID; ESI-MS; MALDI-MS imaging; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes acetyl-TAG accumulation, reaching up to 93 mol% in camelina and 98 mol% in pennycress when combined with fae1 background and DGAT1 RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EfDAcT expression enabled very high acetyl-TAG synthesis in camelina and pennycress seeds. 2. In fae1 mutant backgrounds, EfDAcT drove much higher acetyl-TAG accumulation, especially in pennycress. 3. Combining EfDAcT with DGAT1 RNAi produced nearly pure acetyl-TAG seed oil, up to 93 mol% in camelina and 98 mol% in pennycress. 4. MALDI-MS imaging showed acetyl-TAG in both cotyledons and embryonic axis, consistent with synthesis from tissue-specific DAG pools shared with TAG biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering enzyme for designer low-viscosity oils; best deployed with fae1 mutation and DGAT1 suppression to maximize acetyl-TAG purity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: slightly reduced fatty acid content; slower germination in some transgenic pennycress lines; otherwise minimal growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR of EfDAcT expression; lipidomics by ESI-MS and MALDI-MSI
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EfDAcT transcript abundance did not obviously correlate with acetyl-TAG level among transgenic lines, indicating substrate availability and competing pathways are major determinants

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: designer oil trait via redirecting carbon and acetyl-CoA toward acetyl-TAG
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: very-long-chain fatty acid elongation connected to storage oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation branch competing with acetyl-TAG synthesis for acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: fatty acid elongase 1 / 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase of seed VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: fatty acid elongase condensing enzyme / KCS family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly in developing seed cotyledons versus embryonic axis as discussed from prior evidence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing seeds; greatly reduced transcript in fae1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native gene; knockout by CRISPR-generated mutation background
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seeds under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleoyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA-derived elongation system using acetyl-CoA pool indirectly; relevant competition for acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes elongation of oleic-acid-derived acyl-CoAs into very-long-chain fatty acids used in seed TAG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for VLCFA synthesis in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout mutant analysis; acetyl-CoA quantification; fatty acid profiling; crosses into transgenic EfDAcT backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of FAE1 reduces VLCFA and increases acetyl-TAG accumulation, especially in pennycress where acetyl-CoA increased by 57% and acetyl-TAG reached up to 98 mol% with EfDAcT+DGAT1 RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fae1 mutants in camelina and pennycress nearly eliminated VLCFA accumulation in seeds. 2. Pennycress fae1 seeds showed a 57% increase in acetyl-CoA, whereas camelina fae1 did not significantly increase acetyl-CoA. 3. The fae1 background strongly enhanced EfDAcT-mediated acetyl-TAG production in pennycress and modestly in camelina. 4. FAE1 therefore acts as a competing pathway gene whose removal redirects substrate availability toward acetyl-TAG synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable background edit to remove VLCFA and increase acetyl-TAG yield; especially effective in high-VLCFA hosts like pennycress
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: camelina fae1 slightly reduced seed weight and fatty acid content; pennycress fae1 showed minimal seed penalties and ~2-day later flowering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR of FAE1 in pennycress developing seeds; metabolite analysis of acetyl-CoA; lipidomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: camelina has three active FAE1 homeologs; species-specific efficacy indicates dependence on baseline VLCFA flux

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: designer oil composition by reducing competing TAG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: canonical TAG assembly branch competing with acetyl-TAG formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in developing seeds; reduced in DGAT1-RNAi lines and also reduced in EfDAcT-expressing pennycress, especially in fae1 background
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seeds under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to the sn-3 position of diacylglycerol to produce TAG
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression combined with stable transgenics; RT-qPCR; lipid profiling; crosses; MALDI-MS imaging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suppression of DGAT1 increases acetyl-TAG accumulation by reducing competition for DAG/acyl-CoA substrates, enabling up to 93 mol% in camelina and 98 mol% in pennycress when combined with EfDAcT and fae1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT1 is the main competing enzyme for TAG synthesis during seed oil assembly. 2. DGAT1 RNAi further increased EfDAcT-mediated acetyl-TAG accumulation in both camelina and pennycress. 3. In pennycress, endogenous DGAT1 transcript levels were also reduced in EfDAcT lines, particularly in the fae1 background. 4. Residual TAG in ultrahigh acetyl-TAG seeds was largely confined to the embryonic axis, consistent with incomplete elimination of DGAT1/other TAG synthesis there.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important suppression target to maximize acetyl-TAG purity; should be stacked with EfDAcT and fae1 edits
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: some slower germination in lines with strong acetyl-TAG accumulation and DGAT1 RNAi; minor seed/oil composition changes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR of DGAT1 in pennycress developing seeds; lipidomics by ESI-MS and MALDI-MSI
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors speculate DGAT1 expression may be linked to its own activity or VLCFA substrate availability, but mechanism remains unknown
